content: Just as the human linguistic learning of 'reflexion' as a concept requires repeated encounter across different contexts (like high school English unit exercises) to solidify its meaning and correct usage, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by grounding agent self-reflection in contextual, spaced repeated exposure to past performance errors, rather than one-off single-perspective reflection. This frames autonomous agent reflexion not as a one-step computational module, but as a learning process analogous to how humans acquire the very concept of reflection itself.
